description_long: This street food stall is a compact, mobile wooden cart with a rectangular base and a flat roof, set on a rough, gravelly ground. The front features a prominent red sign with white Japanese characters and a small red lantern, suggesting a ramen or noodle stall. The cart includes a serving counter with a built-in cooking area, visible pots, and utensils, as well as a lower shelf for storage. Metal handles and a wheel at the base indicate it can be pushed or pulled to different locations. The side and rear views reveal a simple, functional design with open access for the vendor and minimal decorative elements. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a focus on practicality and ease of use in an urban street setting.
description: A compact wooden street food cart with a red Japanese sign, cooking area, and serving counter, designed for mobility and street-side service.
description_view_0: Front view shows the red Japanese sign, lantern, and main serving counter with cooking area.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the cart's depth, flat roof, and open vendor access area.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the cart, storage shelf, and absence of signage.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the cart's simple wooden structure and rear access.
attribute_signage: A red rectangular sign with white Japanese characters is mounted above the front counter, accompanied by a small red lantern on the right side. The signage is bold and visually prominent.
attribute_serving_counter: The serving counter is a flat wooden surface at the front of the cart, integrated with the cooking area and large enough for food preparation and customer interaction.
attribute_food_display: No explicit food display is visible; the cooking area and pots are exposed and accessible from the front counter.
